name: OPTIC PATHWAY GLIOMA
definition: JUVENILE PILOCYTIC ASTROCYTOMA = OPTIC NERVE GLIOMA isolated to single prechiasmatic optic nerve anywhere along optic tract ± extension to other low-grade glial neoplasm with cystic components

demographics.Frequency: 1% of all intracranial tumors; 4% of orbital masses; 80% of primary tumors of
demographics.Path: optic nerve is embryologically part of hypothalamus and develops gliomas instead of
demographics.Mean age: 4-5 years; 1st decade (75%); rare in adults without NF1 (GBM); M÷F = 1÷2 to 2÷3
demographics.Associated with: neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) in 10-33-50% (± bilateral optic gliomas)
demographics.Prevalence: extremely rare; 30 cases in this century

dominant_finding: iso- to slightly hypodense compared to normal optic nerve
distribution: posterior extension to involve chiasm + hypothalamus in 25-60% (indicates nonresectability)
discriminating_features: tubular / fusiform / excrescentic well-circumscribed homogeneous enlargement of optic
